libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: No Docker image or environment/system property setup appears necessary. If tests exercise the Jersey OSGi activator or OsgiRegistry, use the real OSGi API types from org.osgi.core rather than bypassing those paths.

deterministic_setup.reason:
com.sun.jersey:jersey-core:1.14 declares org.osgi:org.osgi.core:4.2.0 with provided scope, its manifest imports org.osgi.framework [1.5,2), and com.sun.jersey.core.osgi.OsgiRegistry references org.osgi.framework.BundleReference. The current org.osgi:osgi_R4_core:1.0 test dependency does not contain 
...[truncated]

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8875 [Automation] java run fails for com.sun.jersey:jersey-core:1.14; label=fails-java-run; body_chars=8015

risks: Adding org.osgi.core alongside the existing osgi_R4_core dependency may leave duplicate org.osgi.framework classes on the classpath; local Gradle and Native Image verification should confirm classpath ordering does not introduce a new issue.
jersey-core also has provided javax.mail and JAXB dependencies for optional provider paths, but those are already present in the existing test scaffold and were not the reported failure.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: jersey-core 1.14 includes an OSGi activator and declares org.osgi.core 4.2.0 as a provided dependency; tests that exercise OSGi paths need that API because the existing osgi_R4_core 1.0 dependency lacks org.osgi.framework.BundleReference.
